message:
feat(editor,textarea): add Ctrl+Delete to delete word forward

Implement `delete_word_forward()` in the Editor core, which deletes
from the current cursor position to the end of the next word boundary
(mirroring the existing `delete_word_backward()` for Ctrl+Backspace).
The method respects active selections—deleting the selection first if
one exists—records an undo entry for the deleted span, and leaves the
cursor position unchanged after the deletion.

Wire up the new operation in the TextArea widget by dispatching
Ctrl+Delete to `delete_word_forward()` (previously, Ctrl+Delete fell
through to plain single-char `delete_forward()`). A corresponding
public `TextArea::delete_word_forward()` wrapper is exposed for
programmatic use, with the standard `ensure_cursor_visible()` call.

Add a unit test in `editor::tests::delete_word_forward` that verifies
the basic case: cursor at (0,0) in "hello world" deletes "hello",
leaving " world".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
